reapack.name: ACID Pro Native Grid.lua
reapack.desc: Native clean ACID Pro-calibrated adaptive grid and 24-step zoom

metadata.description:
ACID Pro-calibrated native clean grid for REAPER.
Installs two Main-section actions: - ACID Pro native clean grid - toggle 24-step mode (ReaPack v1.3.4) - ACID Pro native grid - 24-step mousewheel zoom (legacy)
The toolbar mode captures the wheel directly over the arrange view, so no Mousewheel shortcut or Mouse Modifier is required. All grid lines, snapping, clicks and drags are handled by REAPER itself. Levels 0-20 follow the measured ACID divisions; levels 21-23 preserve the measured zoom spans while grid and snap stay at REAPER's clean native 1/1024 limit. No bitmap, custom line, ReaImGui window or floating overlay is used.

version.author: 2TDaSerra, OpenAI Codex
version.changelog: Initial ReaPack release. - Add 24 ACID Pro-calibrated zoom levels. - Add hard zoom-in and zoom-out limits. - Change the native project-grid division at every level. - Add an optional lightweight background synchronizer.

version.author: 2TDaSerra, OpenAI Codex
version.changelog: - Make toolbar toggle the recommended no-setup mode. - Follow every native REAPER horizontal zoom method automatically. - Show the toolbar button as enabled while adaptive grid is running. - Keep the 24-step mousewheel zoom as an optional hard-limit mode.

version.author: 2TDaSerra, OpenAI Codex
version.changelog: - Make the toolbar button enable the complete ACID mode. - Capture Mousewheel directly over the arrange view through js_ReaScriptAPI. - Remove the need to assign Mousewheel in Actions or edit Mouse Modifiers. - Apply the exact 24-step ACID zoom ladder and both hard limits. - Release Mousewheel immediately when the toolbar mode is disabled.

version.author: 2TDaSerra, OpenAI Codex
version.changelog: - Force the native arrange grid to draw every measured ACID subdivision. - Set the minimum native grid-line spacing to 1 px while ACID mode is active. - Make native snapping follow the visible ACID grid division. - Restore both REAPER preferences when ACID mode is disabled.

version.author: 2TDaSerra, OpenAI Codex
version.changelog: - Give the ReaPack toolbar action a unique versioned name. - Prevent old manually-loaded scripts from being confused with the installed action. - Verify that SWS actually applied the native 1 px grid spacing and report a clear error if it did not.

version.author: 2TDaSerra, OpenAI Codex
version.changelog: - Keep levels 0-20 fully native and clamp the native grid to REAPER's real 1/1024 limit. - Add only the missing 1/2048 and 1/4096 subdivisions at levels 21-23 using a single direct arrange-view bitmap. - Snap simple arrange clicks to the exact ACID interval at the final three levels. - Leave drags, time selections, loop points and mouse modifiers under native REAPER control. - Debounce bitmap recreation while resizing to avoid lag, flashing and stale overlay windows.

version.author: 2TDaSerra, OpenAI Codex
version.changelog: - Replace the full transparent arrange bitmap with independent solid 1-pixel lines, eliminating wide vertical bands. - Reinforce every fine subdivision uniformly at the final three ACID zoom levels. - Calculate simple-click position from the raw arrange-window coordinate instead of REAPER's already-snapped mouse time. - Reapply the fine edit-cursor position on the following defer cycle so native coarse snapping cannot overwrite it. - Keep drags, time selections, loop points and mouse modifiers entirely native.

version.author: 2TDaSerra, OpenAI Codex
version.changelog: - Remove every custom bitmap, composited line and click hook from the toolbar service. - Keep grid rendering, snapping, clicks, item edits, loop points and time selections entirely native. - Preserve all 24 measured ACID zoom spans and both hard zoom limits. - Match ACID grid divisions natively through level 20, then clamp levels 21-23 to REAPER's usable native 1/1024 minimum. - Continue capturing Mousewheel directly over the arrange without requiring a shortcut.

version.author: 2TDaSerra, OpenAI Codex
version.changelog: - Replace the approximate geometric zoom ladder with all 24 limits transcribed from the corrected ACID measurement table. - Correct level spans including 58 s, 44 s, 22 s, 11 s, 5.5 s, 2.75 s and the corresponding sub-second levels. - Add one native client pixel after the measured endpoint so REAPER draws the final right-edge marker, such as 41.1.000 at level 0. - Keep the Native Clean design with no custom lines, bitmaps or click hooks. - Apply the corrected measurements to both the toolbar service and the legacy Mousewheel action.

version.author: 2TDaSerra, OpenAI Codex
version.changelog: - Compensate REAPER's 18 px internal arrange scrollbar when applying every measured ACID zoom span. - Keep the measured right endpoint on the final visible ruler pixel instead of behind the vertical scrollbar. - Correct all 24 levels with the same width-aware formula, including 41.1.000 at level 0 and 1.1.045 at level 23. - Preserve the Native Clean implementation with no overlays, custom lines or click hooks. - Apply the correction to both the recommended toolbar service and the legacy Mousewheel action.
